title: DAU / MAU stickiness ratio
template: WITH dau AS (SELECT event_date, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) as daily_users FROM events GROUP BY 1), mau AS (SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', event_date) as month, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) as monthly_users FROM events GROUP BY 1) SELECT d.event_date, d.daily_users, m.monthly_users, d.daily_users * 1.0 / NULLIF(m.monthly_users, 0) as stickiness FROM dau d JOIN mau m ON DATE_TRUNC('month', d.event_date) = m.month
anti_patterns: counting sessions instead of distinct users
joining DAU to wrong month boundary

title: Bounce rate
template: WITH session_pages AS (SELECT session_id, COUNT(*) as page_count FROM pageviews GROUP BY 1) SELECT COUNT(CASE WHEN page_count = 1 THEN 1 END) * 1.0 / COUNT(*) as bounce_rate FROM session_pages

title: Average session duration
template: WITH session_bounds AS (SELECT session_id, MIN(event_timestamp) as session_start, MAX(event_timestamp) as session_end FROM events GROUP BY 1) SELECT AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (session_end - session_start))) as avg_duration_seconds FROM session_bounds WHERE session_end > session_start
anti_patterns: including sessions with only one event — end equals start, giving zero duration

title: Multi-step funnel analysis
template: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN step >= 1 THEN user_id END) as step1, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN step >= 2 THEN user_id END) as step2, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN step >= 3 THEN user_id END) as step3 FROM (SELECT user_id, MAX(CASE WHEN event='view_product' THEN 1 WHEN event='add_to_cart' THEN 2 WHEN event='purchase' THEN 3 ELSE 0 END) as step FROM events GROUP BY 1) t
anti_patterns: not enforcing step ordering — users can appear at step 3 without step 1
counting events instead of users per step

title: User retention by cohort
template: WITH cohorts AS (SELECT user_id, DATE_TRUNC('week', MIN(event_date)) as cohort_week FROM events GROUP BY 1), activity AS (SELECT e.user_id, c.cohort_week, DATE_DIFF('week', c.cohort_week, e.event_date) as week_number FROM events e JOIN cohorts c ON e.user_id = c.user_id) SELECT cohort_week, week_number, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) * 1.0 / MAX(COUNT(DISTINCT user_id)) OVER (PARTITION BY cohort_week ORDER BY week_number ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) as retention FROM activity GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1, 2
anti_patterns: dividing by current cohort size instead of week-0 size
using calendar weeks instead of weeks-since-signup

title: User churn detection
template: SELECT user_id, MAX(event_date) as last_active, CURRENT_DATE - MAX(event_date) as days_since_active, CASE WHEN CURRENT_DATE - MAX(event_date) > 30 THEN 'churned' WHEN CURRENT_DATE - MAX(event_date) > 14 THEN 'at_risk' ELSE 'active' END as status FROM events GROUP BY 1
anti_patterns: hardcoding churn threshold — parameterise or make it a CTE constant

title: Goal conversion rate by channel / source
template: SELECT utm_source, utm_medium, COUNT(DISTINCT session_id) as sessions, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN converted THEN session_id END) as conversions, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN converted THEN session_id END) * 1.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT session_id), 0) as conversion_rate FROM sessions GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 3 DESC
anti_patterns: mixing session-level and user-level granularity in the same query

title: First-touch and last-touch attribution
template: WITH touches AS (SELECT user_id, channel, event_timestamp, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY event_timestamp) as first_touch, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY event_timestamp DESC) as last_touch FROM sessions WHERE converted = true) SELECT channel, COUNT(CASE WHEN first_touch = 1 THEN 1 END) as first_touch_conversions, COUNT(CASE WHEN last_touch = 1 THEN 1 END) as last_touch_conversions FROM touches GROUP BY 1
anti_patterns: attributing all credit to a single touch — consider linear or time-decay models for multi-touch

title: Landing page performance
template: WITH entry_pages AS (SELECT session_id, FIRST_VALUE(page_url) OVER (PARTITION BY session_id ORDER BY event_timestamp) as landing_page FROM pageviews) SELECT landing_page, COUNT(DISTINCT session_id) as sessions, AVG(CASE WHEN page_count = 1 THEN 1.0 ELSE 0 END) as bounce_rate FROM entry_pages ep JOIN session_pages sp USING (session_id) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC
anti_patterns: using page_url without stripping query parameters — fragments same page into many rows

title: Pages per session
template: SELECT AVG(page_count) as avg_pages_per_session FROM (SELECT session_id, COUNT(*) as page_count FROM pageviews GROUP BY session_id) t
anti_patterns: dividing total page views by total sessions when sessions span days — gives misleading averages

title: Event frequency distribution
template: SELECT event_name, COUNT(*) as total_events, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) as unique_users, COUNT(*) * 1.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) as events_per_user FROM events WHERE event_date BETWEEN :start AND :end GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC
anti_patterns: not filtering bots — skews event counts heavily
